report_name: Urgent Patch Advisory: Critical Stored XSS Flaw in Zimbra Allows Account Takeover via Email

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the Zimbra Classic Web Client allows an attacker to achieve arbitrary code execution in a user's browser session by sending a specially crafted email.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The attack vector involves an attacker sending a specially crafted email containing a JavaScript payload to a victim using a vulnerable Zimbra server.

vulnerabilities.name: Stored Cross-Site Scripting in Zimbra Classic Web Client
vulnerabilities.description: A critical stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the Zimbra Classic Web Client that can be exploited by sending a specially crafted email. When the email is opened, the embedded script executes in the user's session, allowing for session hijacking and data theft.

identities.name: Zimbra
identities.description: Vendor of the Zimbra Collaboration Suite, which issued the security advisory for the critical XSS vulnerability.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Exfiltration Domain
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled domain that could receive exfiltrated data, such as session cookies, from a successful XSS payload execution.

indicators.name: Potential XSS Payload in Email Content
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Stored XSS Payload in Email for Zimbra
id: 4f8a1b2c-3d4e-4f5a-8b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
status: experimental
description: Detects common JavaScript tags and event handlers in email bodies that could indicate a stored XSS attempt against webmail clients like Zimbra.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-11
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/critical-zimbra-flaw-could-let-crafted-emails-run-malicious-code-in-user-sessions
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate, complex HTML emails.
  - Marketing emails.
tags:
  - attack.t1190
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection:
    body|contains:
      - '<script'
      - 'onerror='
      - 'onload='
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects common JavaScript tags and event handlers within email content, which could be indicative of a stored XSS attack targeting a webmail client.

indicators.name: Suspicious Zimbra Email Forwarding Rule Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Zimbra Email Forwarding Rule Creation
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4f-4e3d-2c1b-0a9f8e7d6c5b
status: experimental
description: Detects log entries in Zimbra's mailbox.log that may indicate the creation of a new email forwarding rule, a common post-compromise action for attackers to maintain access and exfiltrate data.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-11
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/critical-zimbra-flaw-could-let-crafted-emails-run-malicious-code-in-user-sessions
level: high
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate creation of forwarding rules by users.
logsource:
  product: zimbra
  service: mailbox
detection:
  selection:
    keywords:
      - 'CreateForwardingRule'
      - 'AddFwdAddr'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects logs in Zimbra's mailbox.log related to the creation of email forwarding rules, which can be a sign of account compromise.

relationships.source_name: Potential XSS Payload in Email Content
Suspicious Zimbra Email Forwarding Rule Creation
